prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging and improved diagnosis

burden.mortalityRate: Low with modern care; crises can be fatal

vulnerablePopulations: patients with delayed access to neurology and ICU care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Fluctuating fatigable weakness; antibody testing (AChR/MuSK), electrophysiology (RNS/SFEMG), and clinical response; evaluate for thymoma

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improved with immunotherapy and crisis management capacity

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Antibody and SFEMG access variable; concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Pyridostigmine, corticosteroids, steroid-sparing immunosuppressants; IVIG/plasmapheresis for crisis; thymectomy in selected cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access to specialized testing and ICU/crisis therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Tertiary centers can provide immunotherapy and crisis care
